title: Whisper AI Tool Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Whisper AI for transcription and audio processing.

commands.description: Selects the base model for transcription tasks.
commands.scenario: When you need a balance between speed and accuracy for general transcription.

commands.description: Sets the transcription language to English.
commands.scenario: When transcribing English audio files to ensure accurate language processing.

commands.description: Adjusts the randomness of transcription output.
commands.scenario: When you want a balance between creativity and deterministic transcription results.

commands.description: Outputs the transcription in JSON format for easy parsing.
commands.scenario: When integrating transcription results into a data pipeline that requires JSON.

commands.description: Translates audio to English while transcribing.
commands.scenario: When you need to transcribe and translate non-English audio to English.

commands.description: Enables verbose mode to provide detailed processing logs.
commands.scenario: When debugging transcription issues or optimizing performance.

commands.description: Disables 16-bit floating point precision for compatibility.
commands.scenario: When running on hardware that does not support FP16 precision.

commands.description: Sets the beam size for beam search during transcription.
commands.scenario: When you need to improve transcription accuracy at the cost of speed.

commands.description: Chooses the best transcription from multiple attempts.
commands.scenario: When you want to increase the likelihood of accurate transcription by comparing outputs.

commands.description: Streams audio input for real-time transcription.
commands.scenario: When implementing a live transcription service or application.

commands.description: Increases temperature if initial transcription fails.
commands.scenario: When you want to ensure transcription completion by adjusting randomness on failure.

commands.description: Sets threshold for detecting no speech in audio.
commands.scenario: When filtering out silent or non-speech segments from transcription.

commands.description: Provides a prompt to guide the model on specific spelling, technical jargon, or punctuation styles, effectively acting as a prefix for the transcription.
Enables the model to use the context from previous segments to improve consistency, though setting to False can prevent hallucination loops in long silences.
A debugging parameter used to detect repetitive loops or hallucinations; if the ratio exceeds this, the segment is considered unreliable.
Allows developers to pass specific token IDs to prevent the model from generating certain characters or words that are problematic in specific datasets.
Use the CTranslate2 backend instead of the standard OpenAI implementation to achieve up to 4x speedup and lower memory footprint via quantization.
Enables precise timestamping for every individual word, essential for building karaoke-style subtitles or granular audio-to-text alignment.
Utilizes Voice Activity Detection to skip silent segments before processing, significantly reducing compute time and preventing hallucination loops in silent audio.
Beyond keywords, use this to enforce specific punctuation styles (e.g., 'All caps for emphasis') or to provide a transcript snippet to maintain speaker consistency.
Manually increase the probability of specific technical terms or proper nouns by adjusting logit biases to prevent common misspellings of niche jargon.
Leverage the C++ port for highly optimized CPU inference and memory-mapped model loading, ideal for edge devices or low-RAM environments.
